title: Curaleaf Reports First Quarter 2026 Results: Robust Growth and Net Income
text: Curaleaf announced strong Q1 2026 performance with net revenue reaching $324.2 million, representing 6% year-over-year growth. The company achieved a gross profit margin of 49% and reported net income of $70.1 million, or $0.09 per share from continuing operations. Chairman and CEO Boris Jordan stated: "2026 is off to a strong start across the business...we are well-positioned" to benefit from recent regulatory changes affecting the cannabis industry. Key metrics include: Adjusted EBITDA of $63.4 million with 19.6% margin, Cash position of $106.1 million at quarter end, Domestic revenue growth of 2%, and International revenue surge of 35%. Retail expansion efforts deepened Florida footprint to 72 dispensaries and opened locations in Maine and Ohio. The company completed the buyout of its German subsidiary Four 20 Pharma, filed DEA registration applications for medical cannabis locations, launched its Dark Heart ultra-premium flower brand across 11 states, and secured $500 million in senior secured notes.
